title: rubus tomentosus var. edentatus
intro: Rubus tomentosus var. edentatus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a thorny, deciduous shrub native to the Himalayan region. It is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antipyretic properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and its fruits are edible and can be consumed fresh or dried.
